decisions.decision: Phase 17 planned as 2 plans across 2 waves: 17-01 (pr_creator + hermes_client/MCP status-update, wave 1) and 17-02 (merge_pipeline orchestrator + webhook wiring, wave 2, depends_on 17-01)
decisions.rationale: Mirrors the Phase 16 split (service layer first, orchestrator+webhook wiring second) so the orchestrator can depend on a finished, tested service function

decisions.decision: Declined to run /gsd-secure-phase 16 (post-hoc security audit) when offered after Phase 16 completion
decisions.rationale: User explicitly said 'i dont want to run security enforcement' — do not re-offer for Phase 16. This is distinct from the plan-time <threat_model> block requirement (Step 5.55 of plan-phase.md), which was still embedded in both 17-01 and 17-02 PLAN.md per workflow.security_enforcement=true in config

decisions.decision: Plan files (.planning/phases/17-pr-merge-pipeline/17-01-PLAN.md, 17-02-PLAN.md) were force-added (git add -f) and committed despite .planning/ being gitignored
decisions.rationale: Matches established convention for STATE.md/ROADMAP.md/SUMMARY.md/PLAN.md force-tracking used throughout Phase 16

next_action: Run /gsd-execute-phase 17 in a fresh session to execute both plans wave-by-wave (Wave 1: 17-01, Wave 2: 17-02 after 17-01 completes)
context_notes: Phase 16 (dev-pipeline-integration) is fully complete and committed. Phase 17 (pr-merge-pipeline) has been planned only — gsd-planner produced 17-01-PLAN.md and 17-02-PLAN.md, committed in fff9d86. No execution has started: zero tasks done, zero commits against the plan's files_modified list. The first planner subagent attempt aborted citing a stale context-exhaustion warning that did not actually apply to it (fresh subagent, full budget) — a retry succeeded normally and produced both plans cleanly. Resuming agent should just run /gsd-execute-phase 17 directly; no unresolved planning decisions remain.
